title: EditableSurface registry: V0 allow-list and forbid-list
goal: Define and enforce the V0 editable surface allow-list (ModelManual capsule text + retrieval policy params) and forbid-list (spec text, role-shared system prompts, LoRA weights, tool descriptions) per AC-DISTILL-EDITABLE-SURFACE. Provide an EditableSurfaceProvider that the loop core (MT-148) consumes; any attempt to register or mutate a forbidden surface fails at compile time (closed enum) and at runtime (typed error).
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/self_improve/editable_surface.rs (EditableSurfaceProvider trait + ModelManualSurface + RetrievalPolicySurface concrete impls + EditableSurfaceError + ForbidddenSurfaceGuard). Tests src/backend/handshake_core/tests/editable_surface_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-148 loop core ships LoopTarget enum.
implementation_notes: Per AC-DISTILL-EDITABLE-SURFACE the V0 editable surface is exactly: (1) ModelManual capsule text (spec §10.15) and (2) retrieval policy parameters (top_k, capsule_budget_bytes). The forbid-list is: spec text (shadow-authority risk), role-shared system prompts (blast radius), LoRA weights (no V0 training infra), tool descriptions. Surface: `pub trait EditableSurfaceProvider { fn snapshot(target: &LoopTarget) -> Result<EditableSurfaceSnapshot, EditableSurfaceError>; fn apply_proposal(snapshot: &EditableSurfaceSnapshot, proposal: SurfaceProposal) -> Result<EditableSurfaceSnapshot, EditableSurfaceError>; }`. Two concrete impls: ModelManualSurface (reads ModelManual via the Tauri IPC backed by src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_manual/mod.rs from cluster X; writes via KernelActionCatalogV1) and RetrievalPolicySurface (reads from CapsulePolicyTable MT-142; writes via KernelActionCatalogV1 PolicyTableUpdate action). Forbidden surface guard: `pub struct ForbiddenSurfaceGuard;` with `pub fn check(target: &LoopTarget) -> Result<(), EditableSurfaceError>` returning `EditableSurfaceError::Forbidden { surface_id: String, reason: ForbidReason }` for any attempt that walks the LoopTarget enum into a forbid-list. ForbidReason enum: ShadowAuthority, BlastRadiusTooWide, NoTrainingInfraInV0, ToolDescriptionAuthority. Tests: ModelManualSurface snapshot+apply round-trip via mock catalog; RetrievalPolicySurface enforces budget bounds (cannot propose top_k=0 or budget>1MB); ForbiddenSurfaceGuard fails for any synthetic LoopTarget that names a spec path; guard is invoked by snapshot() before any work.
risk_if_missed: Without an enforced allow/forbid list, the loop can silently widen the editable surface and shadow-authority risk (spec edits via the loop) becomes real.
validator_focus: ForbiddenSurfaceGuard is the only check that runs before snapshot/apply; both impls go through the guard. Closed enum means widening requires a typed code change.

implementation_record.summary: MT-149 EditableSurface registry is on disk (src/self_improve/editable_surface.rs, 20K, authored 2026-05-21 by prior session) with 9 inline #[cfg(test)] tests already covering forbidden-guard variants + clamp behavior. This session added the integration test file tests/editable_surface_tests.rs (18 tests) that the contract owned_files names, covering: guard-before-snapshot proof per forbid-reason (4 tests using read_attempts RefCell to prove read_section closure does NOT fire on Forbidden targets), catalog routing proof per surface (recorder closure asserts the production action_id + write_box schema_id constants), policy bounds at the surface boundary (top_k 0/above-max, budget 0/above-max, empty/oversize ModelManual text), MismatchedTarget cross-surface rejection both directions, standalone guard adversarial scenarios (uppercase spec anchor, /spec/ path segment, legit intro section, retrieval-policy unconditional pass). Post-RFV hardening on 2026-05-25 registered the two production catalog actions in kernel002_action_catalog so the previously documented follow-on catalog gap is closed as supporting evidence.

spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_1_no_deferred_live_escape: PASS — editable_surface.rs is a complete impl with no '*Unavailable' / 'pending MT-NNN' / 'deferred to follow-on' patterns.
spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_2_external_resource_touch: PASS — integration tests exercise the public EditableSurfaceProvider trait via real ModelManualSurface + RetrievalPolicySurface impls with injected closures; lib tests additionally exercise the inline module-private paths.
spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_3_implementer_cannot_self_certi: PASS — implementation authored in prior session; tests authored in this session; both differ from any validator role.

red_team_coverage.forbidden_surface_guard_invoked_uncondit: covered — 4 tests use a RefCell read-attempts counter and assert it stays 0 when guard rejects; proves guard short-circuits before the read_section closure fires.
red_team_coverage.both_concrete_surfaces_write_via_kernel_: covered — mt149_model_manual_apply_routes_through_catalog_action_id + mt149_retrieval_policy_apply_routes_through_catalog_action_id record the catalog action_id and write_box_schema_id constants ('kernel.model_manual.update_section' / 'hsk.write_box.model_manual_section@1' and 'kernel.memory_capsule.policy_table_update' / 'hsk.write_box.memory_capsule_policy_update@1'). Post-RFV hardening registered both action IDs and write-box schema IDs in kernel002_action_catalog, and kernel_action_catalog_tests now pin those registrations.
red_team_coverage.policy_bounds_top_k_gt_zero_and_budget_w: covered — 4 tests reject zero + above-max for both top_k and capsule_budget_bytes; MAX_TOP_K + MAX_CAPSULE_BUDGET_BYTES const values used in test assertions.

residual_risks: Integration tests use injected closures; the closure is responsible for routing the write through KernelActionCatalogV1. A wrong implementation could pass tests by recording the right constants without actually routing. Validator should verify the production ModelManualSurface + RetrievalPolicySurface wiring code explicitly invokes the catalog dispatcher.
No Integration Validator has reviewed MT-149.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Forbidden surface guard is invoked unconditionally before any snapshot or apply.
Both concrete surfaces write via KernelActionCatalogV1 so the audit trail is the same as operator-driven edits.
Policy bounds (top_k > 0, budget_bytes within sane ceiling) enforced at the surface, not at the proposer.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: EditableSurface implements the spec 4.8.5 allow-list (ModelManual text + RetrievalPolicy top_k/capsule_budget_bytes) and forbid-list (spec/role/lora/tool_description) with typed ForbidReason variants; ForbiddenSurfaceGuard runs unconditionally before every snapshot.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: EditableSurface implements the spec 4.8.5 allow-list (ModelManual text + RetrievalPolicy top_k/capsule_budget_bytes) and forbid-list (spec/role/lora/tool_description) with typed ForbidReason variants; ForbiddenSurfaceGuard runs unconditionally before every snapshot.
